just throw shit at the wall and hope something sticks, that's not how it's going to be. Each action
you take, whether that is in business, in life, or whatever, you need to do it because you thought
about it, you know that's going to drive your life forward, and you do it consciously.
It's how having intent, and the feedback loop part is just making sure that whatever you do, you
learn from what works, what doesn't, and you might have heard us say these terms many, many
times over, but I can tell you from everyone, the 14 dudes who I helped hit 100k a day, they all

implement feedback loops very, very well. It's like part of their whole life now, whatever they do,
because it's all like this, right? Most people go through life without actually, you know, they go
through the motions, really. They just go with what other people say, no intent at all.
I ask them, hey, a simple one, right? People want to lose fat, okay? Oh, I've tried going to the
gym, nothing worked. I tried this diet, didn't work. Okay, great, what didn't work about it? What
worked and what didn't work? I don't know.
That's same thing with your creatives, right? Most common question I ask, okay, you're out of
your creatives, what's your hit rate? I don't know. I just tested 300, nothing worked, I think. It
makes sense.
You don't even know how many of them worked. You don't even know what you tested. You don't

How are you then going to replicate success, and how are you then going to avoid mistakes?
You're not going to be able to. That's the main one. And I think, I don't want to add much more to
this, but the only thing I would say is, like, make it a natural process, and this is something that

yourself, what is the next action I take? It's not, oh, this is why this ad doesn't work, and then you
just forget about it.
It's, okay, oh, this is why this ad doesn't work, what am I going to do about it, right? That is the
most, like, I think we've talked a lot about feedback loops, and we've maybe briefly talked about
actioning them, but, like, you love this, but it's, like, you have to action it. Like, that is the most
important thing. You have to create a plan and write something down about what are you going to
do about the thing that you just learned.
And, like, that, like you said, is, that's one of the biggest things that I've noticed, is, like, you can
complete feedback loops and, like, just get this information, and then it just flows around your
brain, and then you forget about it, but the people who actually, like, write down what their plan is

general, I would say would be the one system, and if you guys want some sauce for some AIs, AI

action item and then you test that action item and it works, add that to context for AI. Hey, AI,
here's something that we did, we learned this, this ended up working, here is what it looks like,
and just stack that with as much as you can, and that is going to be, I think, where everything

1.Overview & Self Onboarding
Alright, welcome to the first loom in the market research process. What I'm going to be talking
about here is how to get things kicked off and how to just buckle in for what is going to be a very,
very, very exciting process and probably one of the most important things that you're going to do
in order to start growing and scaling your business. One of the main bottlenecks that I face at the
agency, just to give you a little context before we jump in, is trying to understand why things were
actually working and trying to understand how to come up with new ideas.
I didn't actually get it on the depth of my essentially creative strategy and my ability to create
winning ads. And when I say my ability, it actually was my team's ability as well. I was essentially
Twitter and whatever influencers were posting, whatever hooks were trending that month and
Foreplay.
And Foreplay is an app and we'll get to that very shortly. We're actually deeper down into the
research process of how we now use it. But this research process right here that I'm about to
show you has absolutely transformed the way that we have done business and transformed the
way that we've been able to scale our brands and help people actually predictably grow their
business.
So I know we've talked a lot about psychology, but now in this next phase, I really want you to
take this seriously. And there's a lot of people that say they do research, but I can promise you,
you have not done research until you go through what I'm about to show you in this process.
Now, I do, again, need to give full credits to Nick Theriault for very, very much so helping me, and
also that's a common theme throughout this course, but for very much so helping me better
understand research.
And I'm very excited to kind of walk you through some of the research processes that I have
learned from him and some of the research processes that we've actually created ourselves and
some that I've actually learned from others as well. So with that said, in this first part, before we
get into the research, I need you to do some self-research. And that self-research is actually what
we typically would collect in the form of an onboarding.
But what I want you to do is essentially onboard yourself and start asking yourself some of these
initial questions before you do the research. Start asking yourself some of these initial questions
to better understand how you currently see your business. And by the end of it, kind of compare
that to where we're going to be at after you actually understand the market.
So the first thing that you're going to do is you're going to open up this document. You're, of
course, going to make a copy. And once you make a copy, you will then begin to fill out these
questions.
So very first question here, what is your business differentiator? How do you stand out from the

rest? This is one of the most important things for you to call out and know before jumping into
research. Number two down here, what are two to three things you wish everyone knew about
your product? What are your best-selling products? Again, if you have multiple, what are your

process. And then once you have this filled out, there will be another room down here that

⭐ How To Beat Meta Invoicing Update
Special Guest: Grasyon Cross - March 11th, 2026 - Going through the sauce on how to still use
your credit card to pay for the new upcoming Meta invoicing update
VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/BzN_MEG4tXv5UDkoR7gxZyZcdRm9rB_q
Meeting Purpose
To outline a strategy for navigating Meta's upcoming invoicing update.
Key Takeaways


Meta's invoicing update (est. late April) ends credit card payments, switching all
accounts to Net 45 ACH/wire terms. This change, driven by fraud, forces a new payment
strategy.



The solution is a 105-day cash conversion cycle. This is achieved by paying Meta's Net
45 invoice with a credit card (via Melio) that offers its own Net 60 terms, effectively
stacking the payment windows.



This strategy unlocks aggressive scaling by enabling a negative first-order profit
model. The 105-day float allows brands to acquire customers at a loss, knowing they will be
profitable from rebills before the ad bill is due.



The cost is low (~1% net fee) for a powerful cash flow tool. The fee is tax-deductible,
while the rewards are non-taxable, making the effective cost of this "loan" extremely low.

Topics
The Problem: Meta's Invoicing Update


Timeline: Mandatory for all ad accounts by late April (a 30–60 day delay is possible).



New Terms: All accounts will switch to Net 45 invoicing (30-day spend term + 15-day grace
period).



Payment Method: ACH or wire transfer only; credit cards will no longer be accepted.



Spend Limits: Accounts will receive a monthly spend limit based on historical spend, with
intra-cycle payments freeing up the limit.

The Solution: A 105-Day Cash Conversion Cycle


Goal: Maximize payment time to fund customer acquisition from rebills, not upfront capital.



The Stacked Workflow:
1. Meta Invoice: Receive Net 45 invoice.
2. Payment Processor: Use a tool like Melio, Bill.com, or Plastic to pay the invoice
with a credit card.
3. Credit Card: Use a card with its own Net 60 terms (e.g., Amex Plum Card).



Result: A 105-day payment window (45 days to Melio + 60 days to the credit card).

The Tools: Credit Cards & Processors




Payment Processors:
o

Melio: Recommended. Fees are 2.5–2.9%.

o

Bill.com & Plastic: Viable alternatives.

o

Built: Not suitable; designed for single monthly payments (e.g., rent).

Credit Cards:
o

Amex Plum Card: Recommended. Offers Net 60 terms and a 1.5% statement credit
on all spend.

o

Amex Platinum Card: Good alternative. Offers 2x points on transactions >$5k (up
to $2M spend).

o

Chase Sapphire Ink: Less ideal. Its 3x points bonus is lost when paying via a
processor like Melio, as the transaction is no longer categorized as "advertising."

o

Amex Blue Cash Card: Advanced strategy. Offers 0% APR for 12–18 months,
enabling a full year of interest-free scaling.

The Impact: Scaling Without Capital


Enables Negative First-Order Profit: The 105-day float allows brands to acquire
customers at a loss, knowing they will be profitable from rebills before the ad bill is due.



Example (Joey's Brand):



o

Baseline: 20k customers/mo at $38 CAC → $732k MRR by month 6.

o

Aggressive Model: 25k customers/mo at $45 CAC (losing $6.50/customer) →
$1.1M MRR by month 6.

o

Outcome: The aggressive model generates an extra $400k in profit by month 6,
funded entirely by the cash flow float.

Low Effective Cost: The net fee is small (~1%). For a $38 CAC, this is only 38 cents, a
negligible increase compared to the growth potential.

Logistics & Q&A


Non-US Residents: Requires a US LLC (e.g., Wyoming) and an ITIN to apply for US credit
cards.



Credit Limit Increases: Request manual reviews every 30–90 days. Amex views payments
to advertisers (via Melio) as low-risk, which helps secure higher limits.



FX Fees: Avoid by using a bank with no inter-account FX fees (e.g., Chase) for transfers
between your US and foreign entities.



Agency Accounts: The impact is unknown. Agencies should consult their vendors.

⭐ How To Set Actual KPI Targets for Your Brand
Special Guest: Grasyon Cross - October 22nd, 2025 - How To Set Actual KPI Targets for Your
Brand
VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/BzN_MEG4tXv5UDkoR7gxZyZcdRm9rB_q

Meeting Purpose
To learn how to set effective KPI targets by analyzing customer LTV and cash conversion cycles.

Key Takeaways

Optimize for LTV, not just first-order profit. A $70 max CAC (first-order profit) vs. a $210 max CAC
(90-day LTV) shows how focusing on LTV unlocks massive scaling potential.
Segment LTV by cohort. Subscription customers are often far more valuable than one-time buyers,
justifying a higher acquisition cost. Validate this data with controlled tests before scaling.
Use credit card stacking to fund growth. Combine net-30 vendor terms with a 60-day Amex Plum
card to create a 90-day cash conversion cycle, allowing you to fund ad spend within a profitable
window.
Prioritize creative strategy over daily ad optimization. Volatile performance at low spend ($300–
$400/day) is normal; the highest-leverage activity is creating better ads, not constant tweaking.

Topics
The Problem: Limiting Growth by Focusing on First-Order Profit
Focusing on first-order profit (FOP) severely limits growth potential.
Example: A brand with a $100 AOV and $30 COGS has a $70 margin.
FOP Model: Max CAC = $70.
LTV Model: With a 300% 90-day LTV multiplier, max CAC = $210.

This LTV-based approach unlocks significant scaling by allowing a higher CAC that is still profitable
within a reasonable timeframe.
The Solution: Segment LTV by Cohort
Customer cohorts (e.g., subscribers vs. one-time buyers) have vastly different LTVs.
Example: A skincare brand avoided pushing subscriptions because it raised CAC by $6–$10.
One-Time Buyers: 90-day LTV multiplier of 140% → $53 max CAC.
Subscription Buyers: 90-day LTV multiplier of 317% → $75 profit after 90 days, despite a firstorder loss.
Action: Segment your data to find these high-value cohorts. Validate the LTV with controlled tests
(50–100 users) before scaling to ensure the value holds.
Implementation: Funding Growth with a 90-Day Cash Conversion Cycle
A 90-day cash conversion cycle allows you to fund ad spend within a profitable window.
Mechanism: Combine net-30 vendor terms with a 60-day Amex Plum card.
Benefit: This creates a 90-day float, allowing you to acquire customers at a loss and become
profitable before the ad bill is due.
Tactic: Use Bill.com or Plastiq to pay vendors with a credit card, extending the float.

Q&A: Applying the Framework

Low LTV for a Consumable Brand: A skincare brand's low LTV (20% return rate) was diagnosed as
a lack of a subscription offer.
High Payment Failure Rate: A 40% failure rate on a 35%-off intro offer was attributed to attracting
low-intent customers.
Test: Clone the page with no discount. The higher CAC cohort will likely have a much higher LTV.
Increasing LTV:
Churn Mitigation: Offer a free gift 2–3 days before the highest churn spike (often around day 38) to
prevent cancellations.
Cost Savings: Merge orders for customers with multiple subscriptions to save on shipping.
Apparel Subscriptions:
Model: Offer a "club" membership. Barbell Apparel charges $49/mo for $59 in store credit.
Micro-Subscription: A low-cost ($4.99/mo) membership for free shipping can be highly profitable.
Volatile Ad Performance ($300–$400/day): This is normal at low spend.

Cause: Meta quickly exhausts the "hottest" audience, leading to performance dips.
Solution: Focus on creative strategy. The "Avatar Training" and "How to Plan Ads" modules teach
how to identify why ads work, which is the key to scaling.

⭐ How To Find A-Players - October 8th, 2025
October 8th, 2025 - How To Find A-Players With Kyle Hunt: Evolve Special Guest
VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/vntVNXRfbmP6kzbaFzk8_uxaEe5Fwf3g
A-Player Acquisition Engine doc: https://gamma.app/docs/A-Player-Acquisition-Engine5ld6tk6aa9tp7ny
Meeting Purpose
Kyle Hunt delivers an in-depth masterclass on recruiting A-player talent for e-commerce brands
and agencies.
Key Takeaways


The talent war is intensifying, with a shortage of truly skilled creative strategists and
increasing automation of junior roles



Recruiting is only 20% of the battle; a full talent operating system (recruit, hire, onboard,
retain, ascend) is crucial



A proactive, "always be recruiting" approach can reduce hiring time from 90 to 20-45 days



Job postings should be treated like sales letters, with compelling headlines, video sales
letters, and detailed information



Role scorecards are essential for standardizing hiring criteria and evaluating candidates
objectively

Topics
The Talent War and Recruiting Landscape


~45,000 Shopify Plus merchants vs only ~5,000 truly senior creative strategists globally



Entry-level job postings in U.S. declined 35% since January 2023



Most brands treat hiring as a chore rather than a critical business system



Recruiting is only 20% of the full talent acquisition and management process

8 Places to Find A-Players
1. Referral program: Highest converting channel; offer 5-10% of first-year salary as bonus
2. Website careers page: 24/7 recruiter; keep key positions always open
3. Outbound LinkedIn: Use Sales Navigator or Recruiter; send 100-200 connection
requests/week
4. Social media: Post about hiring weekly, even if not actively hiring
5. Facebook groups: Utilize niche communities (e.g., AdLeaks, Cult of Copy)
6. Niche job boards: Dynamite Jobs, Job Rack, emailjobs.io
7. Big job boards: Indeed, Monster (lowest priority, highest volume)
8. LinkedIn ads: Can work but Kyle hasn't personally used them
Job Posting as a Sales Letter


Treat job postings like landing pages to convince A-players to apply



Include compelling headline, video sales letter, link to socials, detailed role info



List hiring process steps and compensation ranges



Use a PS section for additional sales copy

Role Scorecards


Create before recruiting to set clear expectations



Include position mission, targets, initiatives, skills/traits/competencies, responsibilities,
core values



Use to standardize evaluation criteria and copy into job postings

Hiring Process and Candidate Evaluation


Multi-step process: career history form, HR screen, team lead interview, team interview,
CEO interview



Pay candidates for trial projects to evaluate skills



Move quickly through process (e.g., daily interviews) to keep top candidates engaged



Consider offering unique opportunities (e.g., senior roles, work-life balance) to compete
with higher-paying competitors

Meeting Purpose
To provide comprehensive guidance on Q4 preparation strategies for e-commerce businesses,
covering offers, creatives, CRO, and planning across August to January.
Key Takeaways


Start Black Friday sales earlier (early November) to build momentum and test
offers/creatives



Focus on percentage off or dollar amount off as core offers; avoid gimmicky promotions



Prioritize product photos with offer text for ads; avoid overcomplicating creative
production



Conduct thorough website QA and split testing before Black Friday to optimize conversions



Plan for worst-case scenarios (e.g. account bans, inventory issues) by having backups ready

Topics
Q4 Psychology and Sale Timing


Starting sales earlier (early November) allows capturing early buyers and testing



Momentum leading into Black Friday is crucial for achieving high daily revenue



Consumers seek multiple small "dopamine hits" from purchases during winter months



Early sales don't necessarily cannibalize Black Friday performance if executed properly

Offers and Pricing Strategy


Percentage off and dollar amount off are most effective core offers



Calculate new break-even ROAS when running discounts to ensure profitability



Consider "Buy X, Gift X Free" for gifting angle



Store credit offers (e.g. $66 gift card vs $20 off) can provide higher perceived value



Combine bundle discounts with additional % off for larger total discount messaging

Creative Strategy


Product photos with offer text overlay consistently perform best



Repurpose top-performing evergreen ads by adding sale banners



Prioritize website/landing page imagery over complex video production



Continue testing evergreen ads alongside promotional creative

Conversion Rate Optimization


Conduct thorough QA on entire purchase flow before Q4



A/B test homepage and landing page elements before Black Friday



Clearly communicate savings amount in cart and checkout



Consider hiring developer for on-call support during peak periods

Planning and Preparation


Review previous year's performance data to inform strategy



Forecast inventory needs and secure backup suppliers



Ensure adequate cash flow and payment processing redundancies



Prepare customer service team for increased volume



Have backup ad accounts, business managers, etc. ready

Scaling Strategy


Use surfscaling method to incrementally increase budgets



Monitor 1-day click attribution data when aggressively scaling



Be prepared to quickly reduce budgets post-Black Friday

Meeting Purpose











Use deep research prompts to gather comprehensive brand/audience info



Iterate with AI to generate ideas, then manually select best concepts



AI outputs still require human curation and marketing expertise



Only marketers who can make good ads without AI will succeed with AI



Continue developing core marketing and psychology fundamentals



Use AI to enhance skills, not replace them

Meeting Purpose

analysis, and superior product-market fit



Optimizing listings through strategic imagery, A+ content, and review generation is critical
for conversion



Advanced tactics like DSP advertising and custom packaging can drive customer lifetime
value



Cash flow management is crucial due to Amazon's longer payment cycles compared to D2C

Topics
Amazon Marketplace Overview


Amazon has become highly competitive, especially post-COVID



90% of brands expected to fail on Amazon in the next 12 months



Success requires competing at the highest level with dialed creatives, CRO, and customer
retention strategies

Evaluating Amazon Viability


Use tools like Helium 10 or Jungle Scout to assess keyword search volume



Analyze competitor performance and pricing



TikTok Shop

Benchmark performance across all relevant keywords

Conversion Rate Optimization


Focus on improving images, videos, SEO, and pricing



Utilize virtual bundles to push competitor ads down



Implement brand story and premium A+ content



Consider product improvements based on competitor reviews

Customer Lifetime Value


Use brand-tailored promotions for cart abandonment and repurchase incentives



Offer subscribe and save discounts



Implement custom product packaging through Amazon Ships in Product Packaging



Develop apps or warranty programs to capture customer data for retargeting

Logistics and Cash Flow


Always have multiple manufacturers and conduct third-party quality control



Understand the longer cash flow cycle on Amazon (2-3 weeks for inventory check-in, biweekly payouts)



Negotiate terms with manufacturers and consider using services like Wayflyer for inventory
financing

⭐ Staying FTC Compliant in Ecom - June 25th, 2025
Evolve Weekly Q&A - June 25th - Staying FTC Compliant in Ecom (E.g. Subscriptions) w/Rob
Freund
VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/keq-KUWKCQFWyMwUaxXzPAxNVGb8B_Up
Meeting Purpose
To educate e-commerce entrepreneurs on FTC compliance, particularly regarding subscriptions
and content usage.
Key Takeaways


Subscription compliance requires clear disclosure of terms, affirmative consent, and easy
cancellation options



Using others' content without permission risks copyright infringement and publicity rights
violations



AI-generated content in ads must avoid false endorsements and unauthorized use of real
people's likenesses



Default Shopify subscription setups likely don't meet full compliance requirements

Topics
Subscription Compliance Requirements


Federal (FTC) and state laws govern auto-renewing subscriptions



Key requirements:



o

Clearly disclose all material terms (price, renewal frequency, cancellation policy)

o

Obtain express informed consent

o

Provide simple cancellation method

New FTC "Click to Cancel" rule adds specificity:
o

Disclose charges will continue until cancellation

o

Specify cancellation deadline to avoid charges

o

Detail amount and frequency of charges

o


Provide clear cancellation instructions

California law requires:
o

Prominently located cancellation link/button in account settings

o

Renewal reminders 15-45 days before charge for annual+ subscriptions

o

Notice 3-21 days before trial-to-paid conversion

Compliant Checkout Design


Norton example highlighted as good practice:
o

Checkbox for affirmative consent to auto-renewal terms

o

Clear disclosure of first-year vs. renewal pricing

o

Comprehensive terms displayed near purchase button



Everyday Dose noted as another solid example for e-commerce



Non-compliant examples: NordVPN (small, hard-to-see disclosures), NSUPS (inadequate
Shopify default language)

Content Usage and Copyright


Using others' content without permission risks copyright infringement



Public availability doesn't imply right to use



Tagging a brand doesn't grant usage rights



Publicity rights protect individuals' control over commercial use of their image/likeness



Damages can be significant (e.g., $16M judgment against brand using Joe Rogan clip)

AI-Generated Content in Advertising


AI avatars can't provide truthful product testimonials or endorsements



OK to use AI for stating factual product information



Creating AI lookalikes of real people risks publicity rights violations



Voice imitation can also lead to legal issues

Terms of Service and Arbitration


Well-crafted Terms of Service with arbitration clause can significantly reduce class action
lawsuit risk



Checkbox consent to Terms of Service is strongest for enforceability



If not using checkbox, language and presentation are crucial for court acceptance

⭐ Click Up Mastermind - June 18th, 2025
Evolve Weekly Q&A - June 18th - Click Up Mastermind
VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/vd38H8DHy8CBuftp25WfU8esYm8q_yx4
Meeting Purpose
Provide a comprehensive overview of ClickUp project management system for ad creation and
campaign management workflows.
Key Takeaways


ClickUp's hierarchical structure: Spaces > Folders > Lists/Boards enables flexible
organization and filtering



Key boards: Client Info, Ads, CRO/Landing Pages, UGC Production, Campaign tracking



Extensive use of custom fields, automations, and dashboards enables detailed tracking of
KPIs and workflow efficiency



System allows granular visibility into ad performance, team productivity, and campaign
metrics

Topics
ClickUp Structure Overview


Spaces: Top-level organization (e.g. client space)



Folders: Mid-level organization (e.g. client folders)



Lists/Boards: Day-to-day task management



Custom task types (e.g. ad, page, client) enable targeted filtering



Views and filters allow customized data presentation

Client Info Board


Stores key client/brand information: retainers, spend targets, ROAS goals, brand guidelines,
etc.



Linked to client catalog with additional details like company info, products, competitors

Ads Board



Tracks ad creation process from brief to launch



Key statuses: Waiting for Brief > New Request > Ready for Editing > In Progress > In Review
> Needs Edits > Sent to Client



Custom fields track ad format, type, revisions, time to ship



Automations notify team members of status changes, track revisions

CRO/Landing Pages Board


Similar structure to Ads board



Tracks landing page creation and performance



Additional fields for tracking offers and page types

UGC Production Board


Manages creator onboarding, content submission, and raw ad processing



Uses ClickUp forms for creator content submission



Tracks metrics like time from creator hire to content launch (avg. 26 days)

Campaign Board


Media buyer focused - tracks ad launches, spend, and KPIs



Calculates daily spend targets from monthly goals

Dashboards


Aggregate data from multiple boards for high-level insights



Track KPIs like ad hit rate, time to ship, revisions, winning ad types



Enable data-driven decision making and team accountability

⭐ Handling Roller Coaster Performance - May 7 2025
Evolve Weekly Q&A - May 7th

Meeting Purpose
Discuss strategies for handling rollercoaster ad performance and focusing on controllable factors to
improve results.
Key Takeaways



Focus on permanent customer desires (wealth, health, relationships) rather than chasing
trends



Move up the awareness ladder to target unaware/problem-aware audiences for greater
scale



Test new landing pages and offers frequently, launching directly in CBOs rather than split
testing



Prioritize creating educational or entertaining ad content to capture attention of nonbuyers

Topics
Causes of Rollercoaster Performance


Selling to real people with fluctuating buying moods, not robots



Macro market factors outside advertiser control (economy, competitors, etc.)



Meta platform changes and algorithm fluctuations



Advertiser strategy choices (awareness level, market sophistication, etc.)

Effective Strategies






Target permanent customer desires vs. trends
o

E.g. Men: make money, look fit, relationships

o

Women: status, health/confidence, caring for others

Move up awareness ladder to unaware/problem-aware
o

Tap into larger audiences vs. most competitive segments

o

Turn scrollers into customers with compelling hooks

Create ads that educate and/or entertain
o



Use storytelling to bridge from attention-grab to product

Test new landing pages and offers frequently
o

Launch tests directly in CBOs vs. split testing

o

Optimize winning pages with smaller iterations

Landing Page & Offer Testing


Test 1-3 new LPs per week, with each new ad batch



Use PageDeck templates for quick builds, focus on messaging



Test completely new LPs and small tweaks to winners



Prioritize offer testing - can double business overnight



Above-the-fold content and headline critical for engagement

Agency Workflow & Systems


Use ClickUp to track creative process and performance



Automate status changes to log dates/times



Review 1 winner and 1 loser ad per batch



Focus on 3-4 quality concepts vs. high volume



Spend 1.5 hours reviewing ads each Friday

⭐ 50+ Winning Ads Document - April 30th, 2025
Evolve Weekly Q&A - April 30
VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/jNiQxSJcw7Trsq2_y66zAGnTzKiYMEW1
Meeting Purpose
Weekly Q&A session to discuss Facebook ad strategies and review winning ad examples
Key Takeaways
- Focus on creating educational, entertaining, and relatable ads to scale beyond product-aware
audiences
- World-class ads often leverage curiosity, storytelling, and deep customer understanding

- Improving marketing skills and ad quality is more important than obsessing over campaign
structures
- Testing landing pages and offers is crucial for improving overall performance
Evolve Program Updates
- New overview video available showing examples of scaling ads and improving efficiency
- 3-2-2-2 method introduced for testing landing pages with every batch of creatives
- Emphasis on testing offers and landing pages as frequently as ad creative
Winning Ad Examples
- Video showcased various successful ad types: educational, UGC, static, and VSL
- Key elements: relatability, curiosity, storytelling, and addressing customer pain points
- Static ads can be highly effective when leveraging cultural references or creating "lightbulb
moments"
Marketing Fundamentals
- Success in scaling comes from being a good marketer, not just technical ad setup
- Studying classic advertising and copywriting principles remains valuable
- Daily consumption of trending content (e.g., using Daily Virals tool) helps understand customer
interests
Unaware vs. Product-Aware Advertising
- Unaware ads (educational/entertaining) can be more consistently scalable
- Product-aware ads are more subject to market fluctuations and competition
- Moving up the awareness ladder can lead to more stable performance
Landing Page Strategies
- Example shown of a review-focused landing page for skincare product
- Testing different landing page types (e.g., advertorial, hybrid offer) recommended

Evolve Weekly Q&A - May 21st - Scaling to $100k/day with Daniel

Meeting Purpose
Evolve Spotlight featuring Daniel, who recently scaled to $100k/day in e-commerce, to share
insights and advice with other Evolve members.
Key Takeaways


Rapid scaling achieved through intense focus on creative optimization, learning from
winning ads, and implementing feedback loops



Team building and systematization were crucial for scaling beyond 6-figure months to 6figure days



Success came from disciplined execution of fundamentals (creatives, offers, landing pages)
rather than complex strategies

Topics
Daniel's E-commerce Journey


Started at 15 with organic dropshipping, transitioned to paid ads at 16



Scaled in Swiss market initially, then moved to US/Big 5 markets



Faced banking challenges due to young age and rapid growth



Took ~1 year to see first real success, now doing $100k+ revenue days at 19

Creative Strategy Evolution


Shifted from ripping/testing many products to deeply analyzing winning ads



Increased creative hit rate from ~4% to 20% by breaking down why ads work



Uses Miro board for visual creative tracking and iteration management



Emphasizes understanding deeper customer motivations behind ad hooks

Scaling Process and Team Building


Hired HR manager to streamline recruitment ($2k/month, high ROI)



Team includes creative strategist, graphic designer, 3D modeler, landing page builder, email
marketer



Uses performance-based incentives (e.g. email marketer can earn up to $5k/month)



Focuses on training team members to think independently and collaborate

Key Success Factors


Intense focus on learning from both winning and losing ads



Willingness to invest in education/coaching (spent $100k+ on programs)



Discipline to execute fundamentals consistently without chasing shiny objects



Mindset of pushing through challenges to avoid future regret

Advice for Beginners


Focus on US/Big 5 markets for greater scale potential



Prioritize creatives, landing pages, and offers over complex media buying



Implement systematic feedback loops and learning processes



Be willing to invest time in deeply understanding why things work

Why Post-Purchase Upsells Matter


CPMs continue to rise, making front-end profitability more challenging



Increasing AOV on the front-end can hurt conversion rates



Post-purchase upsells capitalize on peak customer happiness (right after purchase)



Can front-load LTV before relying on email marketing

Math Behind Post-Purchase Upsells



Example: $100 AOV, 70% gross margin, 2x ROAS = $20 profit



Can increase overall profitability by 35% ($7k extra profit per 1000 orders)



Strong, prominent headline





Clear pricing and savings information

1. Product/Offer Testing: Dynamic upsells, volume discounts, complementary products
2. Price Testing: Test different formats (per unit, per year, compared to alternatives)

4. UI Testing: Optimize layout, social proof, text styles

⭐ 9 Figure Native Ads Strategy - March 26th, 2025

9-Figure Native Ads Strategy With Special Guest: Nehal Kazim - March 26
VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/pxJ1qtPDkcR1qnBdi7ZzY1nyu4B_ckdt
Meeting Purpose
Discuss 9-figure native ads strategy with special guest Nehal Kazim from AdPros
Key Takeaways
- Native ads can be highly effective for mass market products (10M+ market size) that require some
education
- Native CPMs (~$2) are much lower than Meta (~$20), but conversion rates are also lower
- Success requires proper attribution, sufficient budget ($500-1000/day), and optimizing for overall
business impact
- Not suitable for all brands - complex B2B products or those requiring extensive education are
poor fits
Topics
Native Ad Basics
- Native ads appear as recommended content below articles on publisher sites
- Major platforms: Taboola, Outbrain, Teads
- Much lower CPMs than Meta/social, but also lower conversion rates
- Can drive massive traffic volume (100k+ visits/day) at scale
Native Ad Math
- Meta example: $20 CPM, 1.5% CTR, $1.33 CPC, 3% CR, $44 CPA
- Native example: $2 CPM, 0.5% CTR, $0.40 CPC, 0.6% CR, $66 CPA
- Native CPAs often higher, but can still be profitable at scale
Ideal Native Ad Offers
- Mass market products (10M+ addressable market)
- Easy to understand but require some education
- Clear, painful challenge for a defined avatar
- Educational hook (e.g. advertorial) works better than direct product pitch
Native Ad Strategy

- Focus on prospecting/new customer acquisition
- Optimize for click-based performance, not view-through
- Start with run-of-network, then optimize publishers
- Test in 7-day windows with $500-1000/day budget minimum
- Use attribution platform to measure full business impact
When to Avoid Native
- Small/niche markets
- Complex B2B products
- Highly technical offerings requiring extensive education
- Discount-focused or aggressive product marketing
- No attribution platform or landing page capabilities

Contact Nehal
If you want to connect with Nehal, please send him an email & tell him you're from EVOLVE:
(Serious inquiries only, must be spending $500k per month)
Nehal@adpros.com

⭐ CRO Overview & Open Q&A - March 19th, 2025

VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/neKQ--m3qxZeJFu3C1KoTKJQbmLMZidv
Meeting Purpose
Weekly Q&A session for Evolve members, covering CRO strategies, team building, and hiring
processes.
Key Takeaways
- Proper CRO testing requires controlled environments and sufficient data to avoid false
conclusions
- Building a scalable business requires systematizing operations and delegating to strong leadership
- Hiring top talent is challenging and requires a strategic approach, including attractive job postings
and sometimes paid assessments
Topics
CRO Testing Best Practices
- Avoid "send it" strategy: making changes without proper data leads to false learnings
- Use split testing tools (e.g., Intelligems) for controlled experiments
- Test for at least 2 weeks, ideally a month for smaller traffic sites
- Focus on above-the-fold elements for biggest impact
- Always optimize for profit, not just revenue or AOV
Team Structure for Scalable Growth
- Four key "engines": Campaign, Conversion, Creative, and Content
- Implement a hierarchical structure with team leads reporting to a growth strategist

- Aim to build a system that can run without constant involvement from the founder
- Prioritize building and systematizing one brand before expanding to others
Hiring Strategies for A-Players
- Treat job postings as sales letters to attract top talent
- Consider paid assessments for serious candidates
- Use headhunting messages to find potential hires from larger companies
- Be clear on role requirements and sell the opportunity, not just the job
- For senior roles, prioritize experience; for junior roles, focus on potential and implement strong
training programs
Ad Testing Approaches
- Test static and video concepts separately in different ad sets
- Don't dismiss image ads; they can be highly effective for some brands
- Native ads can be a powerful channel, spending up to $70k/day for some brands

convert

- Hiring strategies discussed: focus on hiring for tasks below your "buyback rate", move quickly for
top talent

Opensend Overview
- Identity resolution market exists to capture 97% of site visitors who don't provide email
- Opensend focuses on high-intent visitors, filtering for 55+ seconds on site
- Integrates with email platforms (e.g. Klaviyo) and ad platforms (Meta, Google)
- Pricing: $0.25-$0.08 per identity, customers often monetize at $1.50-$3+ per identity

- US-only service, CCPA compliant
Hiring Strategies
- Hire for tasks below your "buyback rate" (hourly rate / 4)
- Options for recruiting: in-house, boutique agencies, traditional recruiters
- Full-time recruiter justified at 1-2 hires per month
- Audit time spent on tasks to identify hiring needs
- Track actual profit to make informed hiring decisions
Team Building
- Goal is to free up time for higher-value activities
- Audit entire team's time usage to optimize roles
- Consider profit margins when making hires, but also long-term growth potential
- Tools like Hubstaff useful for time tracking

better results than generic, lengthy instructions

ROI from influencer partnerships extends beyond direct sales, including brand credibility,
pricing power, and content creation

Sam Parham's Background and 1DS Collective


Former stunt performer who transitioned into social media and influencer marketing



1DS Collective scaled from 6 to 8 figures in 6 years



Three core pillars: talent management, media network, and marketing agency



Team of 30 people, focusing on quality over quantity

Influencer Marketing Strategy


Leverage authenticity and credibility of influencers to communicate brand value
propositions



Customize content briefs for each influencer based on their top-performing content



Focus on creating a "flywheel" of content, potentially using product-only compensation for
micro-influencers



Consider equity partnerships for larger influencers (e.g., Prime drink with Logan Paul and
KSI)

Budget Considerations


Costs range widely: from free product-based partnerships to six-figure celebrity deals



Example: Secured an NBA player for $10,000 when initial quotes ranged from $50,000 to
$250,000



ROI should consider both direct sales and intangible benefits (e.g., brand credibility, pricing
power)

Content Creation and Briefs


Keep briefs concise (1-2 pages max) with bullet points



Customize briefs based on influencers' existing content style



Provide clear examples, potentially including self-recorded demonstrations of desired
content







Develop relationships to better align brand needs with influencer capabilities

⭐ Ecom Finances w/ Abir - September 18th, 2024
Call Recording: https://fathom.video/share/y5YTxvU7yH9Uvd6wRAAfoE41LA2Av9D4
Slides:
https://gamma.app/docs/Evolve-30m-EFFFE-z89106pm5rurjy5?mode=doc
Contribution Margin Calculator - Calculate Profits At Specific Spends & ROAS
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1_bAfR1ePXyrmhTUF1twIaHTkycmINkZeNf0hQIMz2Ow
/edit?usp=sharing
IF YOU WANT TO BOOK A CALL WITH ABIR & UP COUNTING HIS URL IS LINKED HERE:
Abir Syed from Up Counting presented on essential financial frameworks for ecommerce
businesses to Evolve community members.
Key Takeaways:


Proper financial management and data analysis provide a significant competitive edge in
ecommerce



Key metrics to track: gross profit/margin, contribution margin, fixed vs variable costs



Forecasting (sales, cash, inventory) is crucial for scaling and avoiding cash crunches



Up Counting offers comprehensive financial dashboards and CFO services for ecom brands
doing $500k+/year

Topics:
Introduction and Background


Abir Syed: Co-founder of Up Counting, former CPA, VP Finance, and ecommerce operator



Up Counting: Ecommerce-focused accounting and consulting firm



Abir's expertise: Fractional CFO services and media buying experience

Essential Financial Frameworks


Bookkeeping: Categorizing transactions, tracking documentation flow



Reporting: Extracting and aggregating data from accounting software



Strategy: Interpreting historical data, forecasting, and financial modeling

Key Financial Concepts


Revenue analysis: Month-over-month, year-over-year, by channel, gross vs net, by product



Gross profit and margin: Net revenue minus cost of goods sold (COGS)



Contribution margin: Revenue minus variable expenses



Fixed vs variable costs: Understanding impact on profitability and scaling

Financial Benchmarks


Gross margin: ~70% for DTC channel (industry-dependent)



Contribution margin: 10-20% for most brands (can be negative for aggressively scaling
brands)



Fixed costs: Try to keep below 10% of revenue

Forecasting


Three interconnected forecasts: Sales, cash, and inventory



Importance increases with longer lead times and more complex operations

Up Counting Services


Comprehensive financial dashboards integrating accounting and ecommerce data



Pricing: $800-1000/month for smaller brands, up to $5-10k/month for larger, more
complex businesses



Typically work with brands doing $500k+/year in revenue

Next Steps:


Spencer will share Abir's presentation slides and contribution margin calculator in the
Discord



Interested members can reach out to Abir via Twitter or book a call through the provided
link



Community members to consider implementing more robust financial tracking and analysis
in their businesses

⭐ Google Ads Course w/ Kamil - July 26th, 2024
Call Recap: Google Ads Sauce With Kamil Kozar
Call recording: https://fathom.video/share/p4Qs1A1zmPGYsfPzWHK9yp2dwBicwS9b
Where you can find Kamil: https://x.com/kamil_kozar
Google Ads Overview and Comparison to Facebook
Kamil provides an overview of Google Ads, explaining that it is a slower platform than Facebook but
can be very profitable long-term. He compares the two platforms, noting that the best strategy is to
use both in combination. He highlights key differences, such as Google Ads being harder to get
approved for and requiring more time to optimize, but potentially generating higher profits.
Navigating Google Ads Networks and Campaigns
Kamil outlines the main Google Ads networks - shopping ads, search ads, display ads, and the
Performance Max campaign type. He explains the pros and cons of each and how to structure
campaigns to target both warm and cold traffic. He emphasizes the importance of optimizing
product feeds and titles for the shopping network.
Strategies for Getting Approved on Google Ads
Kamil dives deep into the process of getting a Google Ads account approved, especially for the
shopping network. He provides a detailed checklist covering website requirements, product
policies, and other steps needed to comply with Google's strict guidelines. He also shares tips for
duplicating products and optimizing landing pages to work around certain restrictions.
Tracking and Measurement Considerations
Kamil discusses the importance of using multiple tracking solutions, including Google's own tools
as well as third-party attribution platforms like Triple Whale. He cautions against over-relying on a
single tracking method, as it can lead to inflated performance data and suboptimal campaign
optimization.
Scaling Strategies for Google Ads

Kamil outlines advanced strategies for scaling Google Ads campaigns, including creating multiple ad
groups and landing pages targeted at different customer intents and pain points. He emphasizes the
need to carefully exclude keywords across campaigns to avoid cannibalization. He also shares
examples of leveraging YouTube Shorts and editorial content to drive additional traffic and sales.
Recap and Resources
Kamil wraps up by highlighting a few additional resources he can provide, including a Google Ads
Transparency Center tool, a Performance Max strategy guide, and a product title creation guide. He
encourages the participants to reach out with any further questions.
Tools/Resources mentioned on the call
Google's own ad library: https://adstransparency.google.com
Performance Max Guide: https://doc.clickup.com/26428242/p/h/t6guj-1828/d1896a5e0da28f1
Product Title Optimisation for Shopping Ads: https://doc.clickup.com/26428242/p/h/t6guj1972/cee44d86c12e379
Split Testing Shopping Listings: https://apps.shopify.com/google-shopping-feed
Kamil's Free Google Ads Course: https://youtu.be/kIT4tDTUm0A?si=Kiaph1SZbGNzK01_

⭐ Special Guest - Zain - June 5th, 2024
Where to find Zain: https://x.com/NotZainAgain
Call Recording: https://fathom.video/share/ZjyYer1aHk6TxLdwNufzWnGBGQsR9Vj7
Overview of Zain's TikTok Shop strategy Zain provided an overview of his TikTok Shop strategy,
which involves running a private label product (a hidden camera detector) and leveraging a
combination of paid ads, affiliate marketing, and live streaming to drive sales. He discussed the
seasonal nature of the product, achieving 200K in revenue in March, and the importance of having
inventory on hand and fast shipping to avoid TikTok Shop violations.
Affiliate marketing and outreach Zain explained his affiliate marketing strategy, which involves
using a bot to message thousands of potential affiliates daily. He discussed the importance of being
selective with affiliates, approving those with high fulfillment rates, and providing incentives like
higher commissions to top-performing affiliates. He also touched on the challenges of getting
affiliates to consistently create content.
Leveraging TikTok Shop content on other platforms Zain discussed how he leverages the usergenerated content (UGC) from TikTok Shop affiliates to run ads on Meta platforms, noting that the
TikTok Shop content often outperforms content created in-house or by agencies. He emphasized
the value of getting free, high-performing creatives through the affiliate program.

Challenges and best practices for TikTok Shop Zain addressed various challenges and best
practices for succeeding on TikTok Shop, including the need to hold inventory, the platform's strict
shipping and compliance requirements, and strategies for dealing with product deactivations. He
also discussed the benefits of working with a dedicated TikTok Shop account manager once a brand
reaches a certain sales volume.
Key takeaways Zain summarized the key elements of his TikTok Shop strategy, emphasizing the
importance of leveraging affiliate marketing, live streaming, and paid ads in a complementary way
to drive sales and scale the business. He offered to provide further guidance and support to the
other participants via Twitter.

⭐ Winning Advertorials - Zakaria - May 17th, 2024
Call recording: https://fathom.video/share/6scxHvFhsGoCXtgjRyGKC11veu1qr-ic
Introduction to Zakaria's dropshipping journey @ 0:10
Zakaria introduces himself as someone who has been doing dropshipping for the last 4 years. He
explains how the dropshipping landscape has evolved, with it being easier to make money through
copy-pasting products in the past, but now requiring a deeper understanding of customer
psychology, marketing, and problem-solving.
The importance of market research @ 7:37
Zakaria emphasizes the critical importance of conducting in-depth market research to truly
understand the customer's problems, pain points, and existing solutions. He outlines a research
process to uncover the root causes of the customer's problems and design a solution that addresses
their specific needs.
Crafting high-converting advertorials @ 17:09
Zakaria breaks down the key components of an effective advertorial, including the headline, lead,
body, and offer. He provides detailed criteria for each element to capture the reader's attention,
build trust, educate them on the problem and solution, and ultimately drive them to take action.
Leveraging AI and automation @ 1:38:55
Zakaria discusses the potential of using AI and automation tools to streamline the advertorial
creation process, from generating avatar-based videos to automating the research and copywriting
tasks.
Scaling and diversifying the business @ 1:40:47
The group discusses Zakaria's current focus on Facebook advertising and the potential to expand
into other channels like Amazon and Google. Ilan provides insights on the benefits of bringing in
specialized talent to oversee different aspects of the business, allowing Zakaria to focus on his core
strengths.
Recap and next steps @ 2:06:35

The group summarizes the key takeaways from the discussion and agrees to connect further to
explore opportunities for growth and diversification.

title: "(19) Shaun Eng on X: \"Every ecom brand that scales hard will hit a point where their hero product dies out.At some point, one product won't be enough. Your hero can’t carry you forever.Most ecom dudes start looking around at what others in the space are launching. Supplement brand adds protein. https://t.co/9wvoePHRca\""

## Post

## Conversation[Shaun Eng](https://x.com/shauneng)[@shauneng](https://x.com/shauneng)

Every ecom brand that scales hard will hit a point where their hero product dies out. At some point, one product won't be enough. Your hero can’t carry you forever. Most ecom dudes start looking around at what others in the space are launching. Supplement brand adds protein. Bag brand drops a wallet. Makes sense. Same customer. Same category. Build it. Spend three months on it. Launch it. Nothing works. Three months wasted. Budget burned. Still no second product that sticks. Blame the product. On to the next one with ‘’massive potential’’. Meanwhile Comfrt, a hoodie brand, launched a blanket. Nobody saw it coming. That blanket became the most sold blanket in America. 70%+ repeat rate across the whole brand. They just listened to their customers. When the next product comes from customer signal the launch is already warm. The people who asked for it are ready the day it drops. You already paid to acquire them. No extra CAC. Just margin. Caden Lane did the same thing. Baby apparel brand. Noticed everyone buying pajamas as a gift also threw a stuffed animal in the cart. The customer showed them. They just saw it. Built it. Your customer is already telling you what to build next. You're just not listening. Check your reviews and see what people keep asking for that you don't sell. Go through your DMs. Look at what's consistently ending up in the cart next to your hero product. The signal is already there. And when you find it, timing matters. If you’re testing a product close to Q4 you’ve missed out on the biggest launch period of the period. Test in May or June, you’ll have real data by July. Enough time to reorder and be fully stocked by Q4. Your next winning product already exists, your customers have shown you. They’re just waiting for you to take action.

[![Image](https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HJByZv1W8AAutsD?format=jpg&name=small)](https://x.com/shauneng/status/2058272215452307576/photo/1)

With complementary products offers are so much better and easier to make and charge higher prices

would you ask them directly in a pp survey?

title: "(19) Shaun Eng on X: \"The golden age of Ecom is here. Shopify is growing 30%+ every quarter. Brands exiting for $1B+. $100M/year brands popping out of nowhere More opportunity than ever. But also more competition than ever. Yet brands are scaling to bigger numbers than ever. How?Go on the ad https://t.co/49n2hTwiQz\""

## Post

## Conversation[Shaun Eng](https://x.com/shauneng)[@shauneng](https://x.com/shauneng)

The golden age of Ecom is here. Shopify is growing 30%+ every quarter. Brands exiting for $1B+. $100M/year brands popping out of nowhere More opportunity than ever. But also more competition than ever. Yet brands are scaling to bigger numbers than ever. How? Go on the ad library right now. Pure slop. Everyone's ads look the same, sound the same, say the same thing. Most ecom bros think the answer is automating with AI. Better prompts. Better tools. More volume. AI works by predicting words. So all you mfs sound the same. You're not solving the problem. You're getting more efficient at accelerating it. More noise. Higher CAC. And you wonder why your CPMs are through the roof selling supplements that sound exactly like every other supplement brand. AI can copy your ads in hours. Your product in days. Information is saturated. Everyone already knows about market awareness levels and deep research. Direct response alone isn't enough an edge anymore. So how do you actually win? If you aren't thinking about how to stand out before you start running ads, you're preparing to lose. Find the white space. A unique mechanism in your product. An underserved avatar nobody else is talking to. A positioning so specific that AI-generated slop can't replicate it. We were just like everyone else a few years ago. Chasing the same hooks and ad formats. Stuck at 6-figure months with no results. We went deep on the customer. Came back with a basic comparison image, not AI slop, that showed our unique positioning to the right awareness and sophistication levels. That brand scaled to $300k/day by Black Friday. $800k/day the year after. The market is only getting bigger. There's enough space for everyone to eat. There's no magic niche or ad format. The magic is in your ability to be 1 of 1. You can copy Grüns and IM8's products. But you cannot copy the space they occupy in the customer's head. When you think of greens gummy, it's already Grüns. When you think of red multivitamin, it's already IM8. That's positioning. You need to find your own lane instead of running in everyone else's. Tis the way of the chad scaler.

[![Image](https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HIDnIUaXIAAUD2m?format=png&name=small)](https://x.com/shauneng/status/2053925028266115467/photo/1)

[View quotes](https://x.com/shauneng/status/2053925028266115467/quotes)

Right. Stop saying you’re “testing a product”. Product isn’t the problem, it sells, you already know that. What you’re actually testing is angles & avatars Same product, multiple LPs, each one speaking to a different customer. One always outperforms the rest by a mile. Find it,

We’re in the golden era of e-commerce, but 90% of ads have become pure AI-generated slop. Same hooks, same generic comparisons, same predictable creatives. The result? Skyrocketing CAC/CPM, audience fatigue, and brands that don’t own any space in the customer’s mind. This is

$1M Ad Report Review + Open Q&A - Feb 18th, 2026

[https://fathom.video/share/MndhsrzbHAkKswNsnvwLvNZ89UqL8UzQ](https://fathom.video/share/MndhsrzbHAkKswNsnvwLvNZ89UqL8UzQ "https://fathom.video/share/MndhsrzbHAkKswNsnvwLvNZ89UqL8UzQ")  
  
**Meeting Purpose**

To define ad performance metrics and review a new breakthrough ad report.

## Key Takeaways

- **New Ad Definitions:** Ads are now categorized as Breakthroughs, Spend Winners, KPI Winners, or Losers to create a clear, data-driven framework for team communication and optimization.
- **Breakthrough Ad Report:** A new AI-generated report analyzes winning ads across two phases: initial CBO testing and subsequent horizontal scaling in ABO campaigns.
- **Scaling Strategy Validated:** The report proves that duplicating breakthrough ads into dedicated ABO campaigns is effective, tripling one ad's spend from ~$300k to nearly $1M by isolating it from CBO competition.
- **Data-Driven Decisions:** The report provides hard data (e.g., ROAS vs. spend changes, performance volatility) to prevent premature killing of winning ads during natural performance dips.

## Topics

### The Problem: Ambiguous Ad Performance Definitions

- A lack of clear definitions for "winning ads" caused team confusion and inconsistent optimization.
- Initial ideas (e.g., flat spend %) were flawed because performance thresholds vary by account size.
- This led to a deep analysis of over $100M in ad spend, segmented by monthly spend:
	- $0–50k
		- $50k–100k
		- $100k–250k (most common range)
		- $250k–500k
		- $500k–$1M
		- $1M+

### The Solution: A New Ad Performance Framework

- The analysis produced four definitive ad categories:
	- **1\. Breakthrough:** Meets `breakthrough % of spend` & `breakthrough KPI` for a sustained `breakthrough time`.
		- **Action:** Scale aggressively.
		- **2\. Spend Winner:** Meets `breakthrough % of spend` but not `breakthrough KPI`.
		- **Action:** Use the future Iteration Playbook to optimize.
		- **3\. KPI Winner:** Meets `breakthrough KPI` but not `breakthrough % of spend`.
		- **Action:** Use the future Iteration Playbook to optimize.
		- **4\. Loser:** Meets neither threshold.
		- **Action:** Kill.

### The Tool: Breakthrough Ad Report

- The report, generated via Claude from exported CSVs, analyzes an ad's performance in two phases:
	- **1\. Initial Campaign Analysis (CBO Testing):**
		- Tracks spend and ROAS at key checkpoints (Day 3, 7, 14, 30).
				- **Example:** One ad absorbed 73% of its CBO budget by Day 5.
				- **Insight:** Performance is volatile; don't kill ads based on one bad day.
		- **2\. Account-Wide Analysis (Horizontal Scaling):**
		- Compares initial CBO performance to total account performance.
				- **Example:** The ad's spend grew from ~$300k in CBO to nearly $1M account-wide.
				- **Insight:** Horizontal scaling via ABOs provides more consistent spend allocation than CBOs.
- **Key Metrics:**
	- **Standard Deviation:** Measures performance volatility. Lower deviation → more predictable Breakthroughs.
		- **Spend vs. ROAS Change:** Quantifies the trade-off of scaling.
		- **Example:** +61% spend → -13% ROAS (a good trade-off if above breakeven).
				- **Breakthrough Signal:** Spend and ROAS increasing together.

### Q&A: Practical Applications

- **Scaling Strategy:**
	- **Process:** Duplicate Breakthrough ads into dedicated ABO campaigns (one ad/ad set).
		- **Budget:** Start with a conservative budget (e.g., 10% of total daily spend) and scale up.
		- **Graveyard Campaign:** Use an Advantage+ campaign with a high cost cap to give KPI Winners a chance to scale later.
- **Skincare Niche:**
	- **Challenge:** High market sophistication requires strong storytelling and belief-shifting.
		- **Strategy:** Niche down, use quizzes for customization, and balance emotional appeals with logical, ingredient-focused messaging.
- **Low-Budget Testing:**
	- **Context:** A $100/day campaign had volatile ROAS (2.8 → 0.7).
		- **Advice:** Let the test run 7 days. Volatility is normal at low spend; focus on the 3-day cumulative ROAS (2.1 in this case).
- **Google Ads Agency:**
	- **Problem:** Agency uses a 90-day attribution window, inflating ROAS.
		- **Advice:** Focus on Meta for incremental growth. Consult Google Ads experts in the Discord for specific contract advice.
- **Conversion Rate Plateau:**
	- **Context:** A brand at $200k/mo (down from $1M) is struggling with CR.
		- **Analysis:** The issue is likely market saturation from copycats, not just the website.
		- **Advice:** Test the new site, but also improve the product and offer to rebuild a competitive advantage.

## Next Steps

- **Spencer:**
	- Consolidate data from 25+ ad reports to define `breakthrough % of spend`, `breakthrough KPI`, and `breakthrough time` for each account size segment.
		- Publish the Iteration Playbook module to guide optimization of Spend Winners and KPI Winners.
- **Michael:**
	- Request min/max performance outliers in the consolidated report to understand the full range of breakthrough ad behavior.
- **Darius:**
	- Let the $100/day ad test run for a full 7 days before making major decisions.
- **Carlos:**
	- Post the Google Ads agency agreement in the Discord's Google Ads channel for expert feedback.
- **Kevin:**
	- A/B test the new website/landing pages to measure their impact on conversion rate.

2.How To Focus Quiet Your Mind -

Evolve · Evolve

Alright, the next part to this video and the time blocking should solve most of this already but a lot
of people ask me, hey so you can't really focus or you do things halfway, you know you need to
do it but like end up being distracted or whatever. So in this next video I'm going to teach you how
to actually focus and quiet your mind. So after you've done the time blocking, which is the most
important thing, you need to obviously remove any distractions right? Whether that small thing
like audio distractions, you're having your phone out there, so obviously your phone, if you can't
live without it, throw it out, noise cancelling headphones on, if you need to move like a different
part, move somewhere else, like some of you stay with your parents, I would advise you to move
out or at least set some time if they can't contact you because you want to, if you do deep work,
you need to be uninterrupted right? So you need to remove anything that will stop you from your
flow.
Pretty simple one but a lot of you guys don't really do this for some reason, set your discord to
D&D as well, just make sure that there's nothing coming in except what you're doing. Easy one
right? Okay the next one is a dopamine detox and a lot of you guys might have heard it already
but the reason why you can't focus is because what you're currently doing, the task at hand is
boring to you. If for example you were a monk up in the forest, all you did was just meditate on
some shit, I'm pretty sure if like I give you a computer to work, that's gonna be the most
interesting thing you're gonna do but if let's say every day you're playing video games, you're
going out partying, work is gonna be the least interesting part of your day, you'll be less likely to
be interested in doing it right? So I recommend for a lot of things, it's better to go cold turkey even
for video games right? If you play let's say an hour a day, that's still like people say in moderation
bro, that still makes it like the most interesting part of your day, everything else besides that it's
gonna be less interesting so if you want to, you should just go cold turkey whatever you're doing,
that's what I did you know stop partying, stop playing video games in general, that's how I
managed to really like get shit done eventually.
So besides that, some tips regarding that, social media as well, delete all the social media that's
not related to business, for me I only use Twitter now, obviously I met all of you guys through
Twitter, no Instagram if you're not using it for business, TikTok if you're not using it for business
as well, not unnecessary. Grayscale phone, so I don't think you guys can see but everything on
my phone is on grayscale so it makes everything less interesting, that's the main part of it right,
so if you see my apps are like, if you guys can see, it's like grey out and it actually does help
because it makes everything less interesting, like I said you need to make everything less
interesting so work is the most important thing, it's how you make it interesting and how you can
get to focus on it. So grayscale phone, so I use social media, even for some timers as well, even
on Twitter, TikTok etc, I just had a timer for myself so I don't get too distracted on it because
doom scrolling is real, even the best people can get out of it, so same thing with me, I just had a
timer for it.

So that's the main thing, next one is easy hack right, cold showers, people say gimmicky, it does
work, why? When you do a cold shower it sucks and when you make something suck, everything
else you do becomes more interesting right, this whole concept, so if you can't focus, just do a
cold shower, it will suck, get back to your work, you'll find that it becomes more interesting
because it's not as sucky as what you just did okay that's what you do when you really can't
focus. The last two steps right, a lot of you guys need to learn how to do nothing and what I mean
by that, sometimes you have some like free time in between calls or work or whatever it is and a
lot of you guys just end up scrolling your phone and whatever which from what you guys have
learned hopefully, it makes work less interesting. Sometimes you need to learn how to do
absolutely nothing, when you're free just like don't do anything, if you can sit in solitude for
yourself for like five minutes, suddenly you figure out, hey you know what, maybe I do have
things I need to do, oh I need to do the laundry, I need to message this person for whatever
reason and all of you guys get put off from that because you guys don't know how to do nothing,
you guys end up doing scrolling or do some random tasks or chat with people on discord right
and a lot of you guys spend too much time doing that and that's part of meditating as well and a
lot of these like you've seen like all those gurus recommend the tips as well, these are the only
ones I've tried that has actually been beneficial and meditating helps you focus a lot right For
example, if you've ever been diving for example, you only breathe through your mouth right and
that's you have to focus on it and same thing when you go to meditate, you focus on your breath,
only focus on one thing and the point of it is that you force yourself to focus on one thing and
when you drift further away from it, you go back, divert your focus back, so how it works is this,
for me, how I like to do it, I'll just close my eyes, breathe in, focus on how it feels when it goes in
my nose and out, feels gimmicky but same thing for work right imagine when you work and you
just started thinking of a lot of random ass things right, oh I need to message this person or

focus itself is a skill, that's why meditation, it's not just for health benefits or whatever, it will help
you focus on the task at hand, so if you learn how to do nothing, how to meditate, eventually

say but has been very very helpful for me to be more productive in general and it will help you

you guys as well.
You click the ad. You go to it. And like the thing that you want is actually not like a good deal.
It's like 10% off, 20% off. And a lot of big companies do this because they know that sometimes
they can trick you. And at the end of the day, it's like, why would they discount a lot of things? I
mean, they're still discounting them, but they're not going to discount it up to 70% off.
They know that's what people want. And so that's what they go in and buy. So just keep that in
mind that if you are running smaller, and I would say 25% is not even that small.
But this specifically did really, really well for us. And again, we did say site-wide. So that kind of
like assumes everything is 25% off.
But I would definitely, the language that we're going to be using this year is like percentage off of
everything. Because then people actually know for sure. The other thing that I really, you're going

FILE: 2._ The Execution Problem - _ Evolve _ Evolve.txt

2.

The Execution Problem -

Evolve · Evolve

Alright guys, so in this module, we are going to get into the execution problem. And my goal with
this is to hopefully help you guys understand why your ads are failing from an execution
standpoint versus just the actual strategy behind it. Because as a result of you going through this,
your strategy should be really good, right? The only thing that's likely going to be holding back is
your execution.
And so hopefully with this video, I can help you understand where some execution can go wrong
and where execution is really good. And at the end of the day, try to correct your lens again on
what you think good looks like. But again, the most important thing for you to do is you need to
understand that you have to complete feedback loops.
You have to, as a marketer, if you want to get better, you have to look at your ads that you make
and remove any ego and ask yourself, hey, if this ad didn't work, why does it suck? Better yet,
why don't people like this ad? Why doesn't the customer that I was trying to go after like this ad?
Where is that gap? Okay? And sometimes you might have to look at it for 15 minutes. And yes,
you're going to suck at it at first, but you need to start with that. Then if your ad is actually working
and getting some spend and giving you direction, you need to understand why are people caring
about this? What about this ad is interesting to this group of people? Like, why do people care
about this? Why is meta pushing this out? And why are people actually buying? You need to think
from the customer perspective more than anything.
Okay? And so unless you do that, you are not going to get better. Okay? You're not going to be
able to complete feedback loops to understand why things work and why they don't. And if you
want to actually get really good at ads, you need to stop looking at just your ads.
And you also need to go and look at ads that world-class marketers make. Okay? Because if you
can go and look at other people's winning ads that you know are working for them, because
again, the hack to this is to just really go onto AdSpy and essentially break down and sort by
shares, big brands and what their top ads are and what ads are getting the most amount of
shares. It's going to basically show you what the best ads are.
Okay? And you need to understand like, oh, okay, that's what they're doing. And try to reverse
engineer and be like, okay, well, if that's what they're doing, wait, let me look at my ads. Oh, wait,
my ads are not even close to that.
They actually kind of suck. Okay? And so you need to really correct your lens on what a good ad
is versus again, just completing feedback loops on your own winning ads. And you have to,
again, get so into that.
So before I even get into the execution problem, I do have a document that we're going to get into
here. I need to make sure that you are focused on completing feedback loops on your own ads.

And you are also focused on studying other people's ads as well.
Okay? And so let's go ahead and jump in here. So when it comes to your ad, concept, angle,
variation, format, testing method, right? It's the idea of your tasks, how you're choosing to sell the
product, the individual executions of the angle or angles, how you choose to deliver ad, and how
you are testing at Markzware and Sniper. Now there's one other variable in here, and that
variable is execution.
And this is the biggest variable. How do we execute them? Now this here, identifying this, you
could do all of this perfectly. I mean, although variation is essentially to do with execution, but you
could do all of this perfectly.
And if your execution sucks, your ad can still flop. And that's frustrating because it's like, well, I'm
doing everything right, Spencer, but why are my ads still not working? It's a skill issue. But that's
good.
At least you know that it's a skill issue and you can work on that skill. Okay? And so even though
you lay all of this out, your ad can still flop. And this is the part of marketing that so many
marketers struggle with because they feel like they've done everything right.
However, when it comes to the actual execution side of things, they've missed the mark. Okay?
And it is one of the hardest things. Okay? And so even worse, what some marketers actually do,
which helps them stay broke for even longer, is they move on from angles or give up on angles
simply due to the fact that they just don't know how to actually execute them properly.
And so if you want to become a savage and you want to scale really fast, you need to get really
good at execution. Now, a lot of people in here have a base level, you know, writing skill set, like,
and everybody has Claude to be able to write at a, you know, above average level. But it's about
understanding what good writing looks like and how to use the machines properly.
Because someone who's a really great copywriter is going to be able to use AI tools better than
someone who is not a good copywriter. Okay? Because they know what good looks like. And so
at the end of the day, that's where, again, when I mentioned, study other winning ads, right? Take
those ads, get the scripts from them and break them down and be like, what are they doing here?
You've got to learn from those other ads.
And the reason why, in this video specifically, I don't have any of those pulled out, but I want you
to understand that that is the best way for you to learn, for anybody to learn, because I'm not in
your niche. I don't know what your product is for a lot of you guys. And so you need to go and
look in your niche and be like, what are the scripts that people are using at big scale? And what
are those ads look like? And now I know a lot of you do that, but you do that from a standpoint of
like, oh, they're running this video ad with this hook.

I'm just going to copy that. You don't actually look at it from a lens of like, how are they writing it?
And what is that doing to the person? And the reason why I'm a broken record with this stuff is
because the understanding is what actually allows you to grow and scale, okay? I know we're off
topic here, but it's just important whenever, like I need to hammer this down for you guys to
understand, okay? And so at the end of the day, this brings us to the most painful, however, most
important lesson for all of you to know, which is the execution problem. The execution problem is
basically when your angle is correct and you do understand your avatar.
However, the way that you executed the ad, AKA the words you wrote and the content you used,
very simple, right? The words you wrote and the content you used was not good enough to
actually convert the customer. Now, is it more complex than this? A hundred percent, right?
There's editing styles. There's all kinds of different stuff, but I've just tried to simplify it down to,
the words that you wrote and whatever you chose to show visually was just not good enough to
convert the customer, okay? It didn't land, right? And this is where so many marketers struggle
despite going deep and understanding their avatar, okay? They just don't know how to properly
communicate that understanding.
And so now as a result of this training that you've gone through, you should have so many
specifics and like one-liners from looking at all these different sub avatars and their behaviors.
And you'll notice in that training as well, I've said like a lot of the hooks are like, okay, I noticed
that someone doesn't go to, or goes to the gym in the morning to maybe avoid people at the gym,
or like goes to the gym late at night to avoid people. Guess what? You can just turn that into a
headline, right? Go to the gym late to avoid everyone, right? So that's why I've done it this way
because it's one of the simplest ways for you guys to learn how to basically write headlines and
start to write hooks because you're just using things that are relatable, which is the best thing that
you guys can do.
And that's why I haven't gone over the complex. But my point is, is like, as a result of this training,
you should have very specific lines and quotes and sayings from your ideal customer and from
your avatars that you can essentially use to piece together and write your scripts. And again,
because I also have given you guys formats for images, right? I've given you guys those image
templates.
Basically, like those are validated works, like those are winners. So if you use that template and it
doesn't work, there's one of two reasons why. So one, it just didn't work for like your product or
your industry specifically, although I would kind of bet that that's probably not the reason why.
The main reason is probably just because of what you put in it. Like the way that you execute the
words that you use didn't work. And I have a perfect, perfect example of this that I actually wanna
show.
All right, so this ad right here, we actually use this ad, this template here to create an ad for one

of our brands. And we basically put photos here and we had a headline here and another
headline here. And we tested this concept and it didn't work.
And now a lot of you would be like, oh, this concept doesn't work or this format doesn't work.
Well, a month later, literally a month later, someone basically made another ad with different copy
and different images and it scaled to $15,000 a day in spend. So was it the format or was it the
actual execution and what was inside of it? It was latter, right? That format absolutely murdered.
And so you guys are kind of eliminating variables here by using some of these proven formats
that are winning. It's what you're putting inside of it and what you're showing. And again, the
words that you're using and the content that you're using to show it, that's not working, okay? So
you can kind of be like, oh, well, that's kind of nice to know, right? Like if these ads don't work for
you, it's probably due to what you put in them and what you showed, okay? So this is another
good example.
Like this ad right here, I had someone message me and it's like, oh yeah, like this is not working.
And then literally for one of our clients, we have an exact replicate of this ad, obviously not selling
socks. It's a different product where she just basically used this kind of idea of the headline and
use these call-out points.
And now that ad spending $700 a day for them and I didn't even make it for them. I was just like,
hey, just fill in this template and focus on these things. And they made it and boom.
And now this ad scaled them from like $200 a day and spend a $700 a day and spend, right?
Smaller level, but you guys can understand that like this will help you eliminate some of the
execution problem because there's templates here if you guys to literally use that are working at

it's what's inside those.
Now that doesn't mean just as an FYI that you should only test these ads. You can test different
formats. Please do, right? But what I'm saying is, is like, at least, you know, hey, if this ad doesn't
