## Meeting Purpose

A Q&A with Spencer, Shaun, and Karlo on scaling e-com brands.

## Key Takeaways

- **Double Down on Winners:** Focus all resources on scaling a single, proven product. Jota's brand hit $200k/mo with no strategy; applying Evolve's frameworks to this one product is the fastest path to $1M/mo.
- **Invest in Top Talent:** Hire for skill, not price. A top editor (e.g., $3–5k/mo) is a critical bottleneck, and a true strategist requires a $15–20k/mo budget.
- **Scale Winners with ABO:** To prevent CBO campaigns from starving winning ads, duplicate them into a dedicated ABO campaign. Start with a budget of ~10% of the main campaign and scale aggressively.
- **Prioritize Benefits over Mechanisms:** In ads, focus on the emotional benefit, not the scientific mechanism. Prospects care about the outcome, not the process.

## Topics

### Scaling a Winning Product

- **Problem:** Jota's brand reached $200k/mo with a single product and no formal strategy (e.g., no customer avatar).
- **Solution:** Double down on the winner.
	- **Focus:** Improve ads, offers, and landing pages for the existing product.
		- **Team:** The current structure (2 editors, 1 copywriter, 1 media buyer) is sufficient; the key is talent quality, not quantity.
		- **CRO:** Add an LP agency (e.g., Arcade) to build advertorials, which are essential for scaling in the health niche.

### Creative Strategy & Ad Performance

- **Winning Ad Definition:** A "winning ad" is one that can scale, defined by:
	- Spend Breakthrough
		- KPI Breakthrough
		- Both
- **Scaling Winners with ABO:**
	- **Problem:** CBO campaigns can starve winning ads of spend.
		- **Solution:** Duplicate a proven winner (after 7 days at KPI) into a new, dedicated ABO campaign.
		- **Budget:** Start at ~10% of the main campaign's daily budget.
		- **Rationale:** This forces budget to the ad, preventing it from being outcompeted by new creatives.
- **Axon Creative Specs:**
	- Use 9x16 video format.
		- Create separate, animated end cards in the Axon platform.
- **Promo Campaigns:**
	- Only effective for *actual* limited-time offers.
		- Running back-to-back sales with the same offer trains customers to wait.
- **RIPs (Re-purposed Ads):**
	- **Problem:** Darius considered using a competitor's RIP to quickly enter a market.
		- **Solution:** Create custom ads instead. Differentiation is critical for long-term success, and custom creative can be produced quickly.

### Team & Hiring

- **Hiring Editors:**
	- **When:** At $150k/mo, Mohammed should hire an editor to free up time for strategy.
		- **Investment:** A top editor is a critical asset, not a commodity. Budget $3–5k/mo.
- **Hiring Strategists:**
	- **Budget:** A true e-com strategist requires a $15–20k/mo budget.
		- **Process:** Hiring is sales. Use a 3-step process (Interviewer → Subject Matter Expert → Final) and ask for references.
- **Team Learning Sessions:**
	- **Problem:** Kevin's team is passive in learning calls.
		- **Solution:** Make sessions interactive. Have team members prepare and present their own ad learnings to build their analytical skills and accountability.

### Business Focus & Mindset

- **Focus on the Main Brand:**
	- **Problem:** Mohammed splits time between a main brand and cash-flow general stores.
		- **Solution:** Consolidate focus. Time is the most valuable resource. Either delegate the general stores to a VA or shut them down to accelerate the main brand's growth.
- **Prioritize Benefits over Mechanisms:**
	- **Problem:** Ernesto's ads focus on a scientific mechanism, but performance is flat.
		- **Solution:** Shift focus to the emotional benefit. Prospects care about the outcome, not the process. Simplify the science or test a new brand concept.
- **Mindset Shift:**
	- **Problem:** Carlos is concerned about the minor impact of turning off underperforming ads.
		- **Solution:** Focus on high-leverage activities (e.g., creating breakthrough ads) that can 10x the business, not minor media buying optimizations.

### Technical & Platform Issues

- **Google Ads Domain Restriction:**
	- **Problem:** Ruben's entire supplement domain was restricted after one product was flagged.
		- **Solution:** Isolate the issue.
		1. **Test the domain:** Run a low-spend ad to a simple page on the domain to confirm it's the source of the flag.
				2. **Test a new LP:** If the domain is the issue, use a new landing page on a different subdomain.
				3. **Appeal:** If the products are compliant, appeal the decision with Google support.
- **One-Time Purchase Products:**
	- **Problem:** Mohammed sells a product with no repeat purchase potential.
		- **Solution:** Build long-term value through:
		1. **Word-of-Mouth:** Create an exceptional product that customers recommend.
				2. **Product Line Expansion:** Launch complementary products under the same brand (e.g., Ridge Wallet).

---
## Meeting Purpose

Weekly Q&A on e-commerce strategy, ad management, and brand scaling.

## Key Takeaways

- **Ad Management:** Move underperforming ads with high spend to an ABO campaign to control budget and test viability. Disable automations that kill ads based on Meta data, as it often misses true performance (e.g., NorthBeam shows a 1x ROAS is actually profitable).
- **Brand Scaling:** For distinct avatars (e.g., men vs. women), launch a separate brand. This avoids diluting the core brand's positioning and allows for a dedicated, more effective marketing strategy.
- **Introductory Offers:** Use free product (BOGO) instead of discounts for intro offers. Free product has better COGS, avoids rebill issues, and attracts higher-quality customers than discounts.
- **Essential Automations:** Implement two ad account automations to prevent catastrophic loss: 1) pause ads if spend increases 5x in 24 hours, and 2) pause ads if the URL does not equal your website.

## Topics

### Ad Management & Optimization

- **Problem:** An ad with high spend has negative ROAS.
- **Solution:** Move the ad to a separate ABO campaign.
	- **Rationale:** This isolates the ad to control its budget and test its performance in a more controlled environment.
		- **Next Step:** If it performs well, reintroduce it to the main CBO campaign.
- **Problem:** An automation kills ads that spend below KPI for a week.
- **Solution:** Disable the automation.
	- **Rationale:** Meta's data is often inaccurate. A 1x ROAS in Meta might be profitable when viewed through a third-party analytics tool like NorthBeam, which captures a more complete customer journey.

### Iterating on a Breakthrough Ad

- **Problem:** A static ad is a breakthrough success, capturing 80% of spend.
- **Solution:** Iterate on the ad's core message and format.
	- **Process:**
		1. **Identify Core Elements:** Pinpoint the ad's key angle, mechanism, authority, and avatar.
				2. **Test Formats:** Create new versions in different formats (e.g., video with voiceover) to see if a new medium communicates the message more effectively.
				3. **Test Messaging:** Rephrase the core message in new ways while communicating the *same* value proposition.
		- **Goal:** Find new combinations of message and format that resonate with the same or a broader audience.

### Scaling with Multiple Avatars

- **Problem:** A brand with a male-dominated customer base (70–80%) wants to launch a line for women.
- **Solution:** Launch a separate brand.
	- **Rationale:**
		- **Positioning:** A dedicated brand avoids diluting the core brand's identity and allows for a stronger, female-specific positioning from day one.
				- **Simplicity:** Separating brands simplifies operations and marketing.
				- **Meta Ads:** A separate brand allows for dedicated campaigns to target women, which is necessary as Meta's algorithm often favors male audiences.
		- **Recommendation:** Start with separate landing pages and URLs under the same Shopify account to test the concept. If successful, scale into a fully independent brand.

### Introductory Offers & MRR Strategy

- **Framework:** Treat the first purchase as an introductory offer to onboard a customer into a monthly recurring revenue (MRR) cycle.
- **Recommendation:** Use free product (e.g., BOGO) instead of discounts for intro offers.
	- **Rationale:**
		- **COGS:** Free product is often more favorable for cost of goods sold.
				- **Rebill Cycle:** Avoids complications with rebill pricing that discounts can cause.
				- **Customer Quality:** Discounts tend to attract lower-quality, price-sensitive customers.
- **Goal:** Transition customers to a standard monthly subscription as quickly as possible.
	- **Example:** For a product like creatine, offer a BOGO intro offer, then guide customers to a standard monthly subscription with documentation on an increased "loading phase" dosage for the first 30 days.

### Kids' MRR Brands

- **General Rule:** Kids' MRR brands are difficult to sustain because parents often find them to be an extra chore.
- **Exception:** Brands solving specific, high-stakes problems for parents can succeed.
	- **Example:** Eczema supplements for kids (ages 8–18).
		- **Rationale:**
		- **High-Stakes Problem:** Eczema is a significant source of self-consciousness for kids, creating a strong emotional driver for parents to find a solution.
				- **Marketing Angle:** Frame the product as a solution to the child's self-consciousness, not just a health issue.
				- **Target Audience:** Parents who are often frustrated with conventional medical advice.

**The main learning from this video:**  
Winning and growing consistently come from managing your downside. We don't want high and low swings. But higher highs, and higher lows.

- What to know and what to avoid to be able to keep growing as a person and your business YoY for the next few decades

> 3 campaigns (per offer)

> 3\. Graveyard campaign: All “losers” from testing go in here under strict bid cap. Surprising how much spend this will get. Insures no creative you’ve paid for goes to waste unnecessarily.

Stop throwing away your failed creatives.

We’ve been using this 'Zombie Campaign' strategy to bring dead ads back to life at Structured and it works wayyy more often than you’d think

Save for later..

FILE: Q_A _ Working Harder Than Ever - Feb 11th_ 2026 - _ Evolve Call Recordings.txt
======================================================================

---
title: "Q&A + Working Harder Than Ever - Feb 11th, 2026 - 🎥 Evolve Call Recordings"
source: "file:///C:/Users/samir/Desktop/EVOLVE~1/EVOLVE~1/51058~1.EVO/29593~1.202/5Q&AWO~1.HTM"
author:
published:
created: 2026-06-15
description: "Meeting Purpose Weekly Q&A on e-commerce strategy, ads, and scaling. Key Takeaways - Scaling Plateaus Are Creative Plateaus: The primary bottleneck for scaling"
tags:
  - "clippings"
---
## Meeting Purpose

Weekly Q&A on e-commerce strategy, ads, and scaling.

## Key Takeaways

- **Scaling Plateaus Are Creative Plateaus:** The primary bottleneck for scaling past $5k/day is a lack of new creative angles. The solution is to analyze winning ads to understand *why* they work and apply those patterns to new creatives.
- **Targeting Is Creative-Driven:** Meta's targeting is based on analyzing ad creative (visuals, copy, landing page), not just audience settings. To reach a specific demographic, create ads that speak directly to them.
- **Positioning Is the Core Strategy:** For saturated products, success requires a unique position based on a niche avatar or a superior mechanism. This is the foundation for a compelling offer.
- **New Modules & Tools Are Coming:** Spencer and Sean are launching new modules (copywriting, supply chain) and tools (Winning Ads GPT, Ad Review Chatbot, new ClickUp system) to support member growth.

## Topics

### Scaling & Creative Plateaus

- **Problem:** A brand hit a plateau at $5k/day after scaling from $2k/day. The US market is declining while worldwide campaigns remain strong.
- **Diagnosis:** The issue is a lack of new creative angles, not the landing page.
	- **Why:** The US market is more competitive, requiring constant creative innovation.
- **Solution:** Develop pattern recognition to understand *why* ads work.
	- **Process:**
		1. Analyze winning worldwide ads to identify commonalities.
				2. Bridge the gap between those patterns and current US ads.
		- **Rationale:** The skill gap is in pattern recognition, which is developed through high-volume ad review and creation.

### Ad & Landing Page Strategy

- **Creative Testing:**
	- **Structure:** Use one CBO campaign for testing unless objectives (e.g., product, avatar) are different.
		- **Budget Allocation:** Budget / Target CPA = Max Ad Sets.
		- **Example:** $1k/day budget / $50 CPA = 20 ad sets.
- **Ad Spy Tools:**
	- **AdSpy:** Essential for validating winning ads by filtering for high shares.
		- **Atria:** Useful for brand research (creative mix, top LPs) but lacks share-based filtering.
- **Landing Page (LP) Strategy:**
	- **Testing Cadence:**
		- **Iterations:** 2–4 per week (with a CRO person + dev).
				- **New LPs:** 1 per week (with one person).
		- **Process for New Angles:**
		1. Duplicate the highest-converting control LP.
				2. Optimize all elements (messaging, reviews, offer) for the new angle.
				3. Send traffic from relevant ads to the new LP.
- **Low-Converting Ads:**
	- **Problem:** High CTR but zero sales on a $150/day budget.
		- **Diagnosis:** The ad gives interesting info but fails to create urgency or connect the problem to the product.
		- **Solution:**
		1. **Niche Down:** Focus on a single sub-avatar to improve conversion data for Meta.
				2. **Use a Pre-Sell LP:** Send traffic to an advertorial or listicle to build urgency before the product page.
				3. **Test a Higher Price:** A BOGO offer at a "super cheap" price can appear suspicious.

### Product & Brand Strategy

- **Product Testing:**
	- **Initial Approach:** Take "big swings" with broad creative tests to find direction.
		- **Positioning:** Success in saturated markets requires a unique position based on:
		- A niche avatar.
				- A superior mechanism (e.g., gummy formula for a supplement).
- **International Expansion:**
	- **Decision:** If supply chain is easy, test new countries (e.g., AU/NZ) with minimal effort.
- **Onboarding Senior Hires:**
	- **Process:**
		1. **Connect with Team:** Have the new hire meet with existing team members for ground-level context.
				2. **Frequent Check-ins:** Schedule daily or every-other-day huddles for the first week.
				3. **Set Expectations:** The new hire must come prepared with questions.

### Evolve Program Updates

- **Context:** Recent travel and event planning (Marrakesh) caused delayed responses.
- **Upcoming Modules:**
	- **Wei Shaun:** Supply Chain (negotiation, QC).
		- **Spencer:** Copywriting.
- **Upcoming Tools:**
	- Winning Ads Custom GPT.
		- Ad Review Chatbot (Apex beta first).
		- New ClickUp System.
		- Updated Growth Guide.

<audio><source type="audio/mpeg"></audio>

It means they're working for everyone else too.

You have no competitive edge.

Time is your enemy.

The more time goes on

The worse your strategy gets

Until it dies.

Where if you just stop looking for the easy way out

And build a real skillset

Not only do you gain an edge because most people don't want to do hard shit

Time compounds in your favor

FILE: Statics vs Videos _ Pre-Landers _Advertorials.txt
======================================================================

---
title: "Statics vs Videos + Pre-Landers (Advertorials)"
source: "https://www.skool.com/origins/statics-vs-videos-pre-landers-advertorials"
author:
published:
created: 2026-06-28
description: "Body – Context Hey Guys, Since joining Origins (and even before), I’ve been testing static ads, but I’ve never really gotten results from them. On the other han"
tags:
  - "clippings"
---
[Icmon L](https://www.skool.com/@igor-l-6158?g=origins)

Body – Context

Hey Guys, Since joining Origins (and even before), I’ve been testing static ads, but I’ve never really gotten results from them. On the other hand, videos have worked better for me.The video structure that performed best was:

- Hook + callout
- Short, relatable problem
- Product intro with authority
- Clear explanation of the unique mechanism
- Desired outcome
- Results + comparison vs competitor
- Soft CTA

The main thing I noticed from feedback loops is that the logical explanation of WHY the product works better (and how the mechanism is different) is what helped hold attention and convert.

TL;DR

Videos work for me because I can explain the unique mechanism and how my product is different from other solutions they tried. Statics don’t, because I can’t explain it well. I’m considering using a pre-lander (advertorial) to solve this.

My Actual Questions

1. If my product relies heavily on logic + mechanism explanation, does it make sense to use a pre-lander with statics?
2. Considering I already hit ~$1k/day but had to downscale, does it make sense to test pre-landers now, or should I wait until I’m back at that spend level and now i should just send straight to PDP?
3. Is the idea that pre-landers are better only at $1k/day+ still valid, or is there flexibility depending on the product and angle?

Appreciate any feedback 🙏

4

<audio><source type="audio/mpeg"></audio>

<audio><source type="audio/mpeg"></audio>

🆕 The Execution Problem

36:30

FILE: Why You Have Rollercoaster Performance.txt
======================================================================

Roller Coaster Ad Performance
🍝’d Edition

What is roller coaster
performance

1 Day:

The Very Next Day

The Day After…

Which leads to
serious cases of…

Copium

Reason #1

We Don’t Sell To Robots
Something we can’t control

Robots
• We sell to real people.
• We often times forget behind the metrics of:
CPC, AOV, ROAS, CVR → Those are all real people
• Sometimes people are just not in a buying mood
Paid taxes, bought gifts, had an incident happen, etc.

Reason #2

The Market Itself
Something we can’t control

The Market
•

The market itself is unsteady and people are scared (stocks, crypto
etx)

•

Policy changes, Natural disasters, interest rates, “Tweets” …

•

A new competitor comes onto the market with a better mechanism

•

A competitor launches a new offer that beats you

Reason #3

Meta Devs
Something we can’t control

Meta Devs

Meta Devs

Meta Devs

Actually You

Reason #4

Your Strategy
Something we CAN control

Your Ads

Your Ads

Your Ads

If You Are Running Product
Aware Ads

In a sophisticated market

Or on a trending desire

Your performance will be on
a roller coaster

Roller Coaster
Problems

• You rely on people being ready to buy in your market.
• You rely on people being ON the platform that day
• You rely on Meta serving YOUR ad to the right person that day
• You rely on existing competitors NOT launching any new offers
• Your rely on NEW competitors not launching a new solution
• You go with the ebbs & flows of the market

The Hopium Strategy

Here is the solution

#1 - Permanent Desires

Permanent Desires

Permanent Desires
Solutions
If you’re jumping from trend to trend, tap into the more STABLE desires that never go away
(don’t cancel me for this)
•

Men:

Wealth = Make more money, get status… Get more girls.
Health = Look Jacked… Get more girls
Relationships = Get more girls.
•

Women:

Wealth = Get more status from others
Health = Be confident in yourself, love yourself
Relationships = Help kids, family, loved ones,

Ask yourself, how can “X”
improve “Avatars” wealth,
health, or relationships?

Then work backwards

#2 - Awareness Levels

Remember This?

Remember This?

You need to turn scrollers
into customers

You need to take someone
with 0 intention on buying

And get them to BUY.

How?

Scrollers > Customers
Solutions

• Climb up market (target problem aware/unaware)
• Create ads that educate scrollers (storytelling)
• Create ads that can entertain scrollers (storytelling)
• Create funnels that continue on the customer journey
• Create offers that are too good to pass up on TODAY.

Climbing Up Market
Most Aware: Save 20% On Humidifiers
Product Aware: The world first stainless steel humidifier
Solution Aware: Traditional Humidifier bread mold, ours eliminates it.
Problem Aware: Waking Up Tired? It’s Not Your Mattress…
Unaware: You’ll Never Guess What’s Floating in Your Air Right Now

Education
• Do the research to educate yourself
• Share those findings with the audience
• When you learn about your product, what it ACTUALLY does,
and the functional outcomes it provides, the more you can
scale

Entertainment
• There is never too long, only too boring
• Your customer will binge watch a lot
• Study videos that are incredibly entertaining
• Every time a video hooks you in, save it.
• Ask yourself: “Why am I watching this?”
• Usually there is “Suspense” and you are
wanting to see “what happens at the end”.

Congruent Funnels
• Some people are not ready to buy RIGHT AWAY… So, they just need
MORE INFORMATION. That is all.
• Sending directly to a “buy now page” is not always best practice.

Oﬀers

How we have been
“Coping” with a real client

Week 1

Week 2

Week 3

Week 4

Week 6
Stable

Week 7
Bangin

Week 8

Week 9
We’re so back

What we did

What we did
• We found 3 new winning ads
• We found 1 new winning offer
• We found 1 new winning LP
• Spent extra time processing learnings (which lead to #22)

What we didn’t do

What we didn’t do
• Test a new media buying strategy
• Test a new product
• Change our creative testing process
• Complain that performance sucks

Focus on what you can
control.

Make better ads

Make better oﬀers

Make better pages

The way
